description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with variables DOCKER_REGISTRY, IMAGE_TAG and NIGHTLY_IMAGE_TAG, the docker:dind service, and stages build_phase0, nightly_build_phase1, build_phase1, build_phase2, test_phase1, retag_phase1 and deploy. In build_phase0 run build-container-docker using docker:stable to docker login, then docker build -t "${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/docker:${NIGHTLY_IMAGE_TAG}" docker/docker and docker push the same image, only on schedules. In nightly_build_phase1 run build-centos-builder, build-centos-kubectl, build-container-slate and build-container-postgres using ${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/docker:${NIGHTLY_IMAGE_TAG} with the shared before_script (docker login), each building their respective images (golang, golang-linter, kubectl, slate, postgres) via docker build commands and pushing them, all triggered only by schedules. In build_phase1 execute build-core, build-webserver, build-frontend, build-documentation, build-shell, build-container-cypress, global-linting and all retag-container-* jobs; core/webserver use the golang image with before_script (mkdir/ln), then cd to the project and run go build -race -o "out/${ARTIFACT_NAME}", frontend uses node:latest to run npm install and npm run build, documentation runs docker-compose -f docker/slate/docker-compose.yml run app build, shell and cypress build their containers with docker build and push, global-linting runs gometalinter.v2 ./... --vendor, and each retag job pulls "${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}", tags it as latest and as ${CI_PIPELINE_IID}, and pushes both, all using the shared docker login before_script and scheduled triggers (or master for retag-container-postgres and retag-container-webserver). In test_phase1 run test-e2e-cypress (docker-compose up -d then docker-compose run cypress service run with baseUrl) and test-shell (docker pull shell image then docker-compose up), both with artifacts and always execution, and finally deploy_prod runs on master using the ${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/kubectl image to substitute environment variables in infrastructure/k8sconfigs, apply them to the staging namespace, and set the production environment URL.
